install.title: Download Overleap Client
install.autoDownloadCountdown: Download will start automatically in {seconds} seconds
install.checkDownloadLocation: Please check your downloads folder for the installation file
install.runInstaller: Please run the downloaded installer to complete installation
install.downloadFailedMessage: Auto download failed, please try manual download.
install.helpBrowserBlock: • If your browser blocks the download, please allow downloads from this site
install.helpWindowsInstall: • On Windows, you may need to allow the app through Windows Defender
install.helpMacosInstall: • On macOS, you may need to allow the app in Security & Privacy settings
install.helpSecurityWarning: • If you see a security warning, our app is safe - this is because we haven't yet implemented code signing
install.metaDescription: Download Overleap k2 client for Windows, macOS, iOS, Android. k2cc adaptive rate control — full speed stealth tunnel through heavy packet loss.

heroTitle.router: Run the k2 gateway on your router

windows.2: If 'Windows protected your PC' appears, click 'More info' then 'Run anyway'
windows.3: Follow the prompts to complete installation. Admin privileges required on first launch.

macos.2: Follow the prompts to complete installation
macos.3: On first launch, allow the system extension in System Settings then Privacy and Security

linux.1: Install via script: curl -fsSL https://overleap.io/i/k2 | sudo bash
linux.2: The script downloads the k2 binary and registers the kaitu.service systemd unit
linux.3: After installation, open http://127.0.0.1:1777 in your browser to sign in

android.1: Download the APK file. If the browser warns, tap 'Keep'
android.2: Turn on Airplane Mode before installing (prevents security cloud scan blocking)
android.3: Open the APK → Allow 'Install unknown apps' → Install
android.4: Turn off Airplane Mode after installation
android.5: Open Overleap and allow VPN permission

browserBlock.question: Browser blocked the download?
browserBlock.answer: Some browsers block .exe or .pkg downloads by default. Click 'Keep' or 'Download anyway' in the browser download bar. In Chrome, visit chrome://downloads to find blocked files.

edgeBlock.question: Edge blocked the download?
edgeBlock.answer: Edge may warn that the file 'is not commonly downloaded'. Click the ··· menu next to the file, then select 'Keep'.

chromeBlock.question: Chrome blocked the download?
chromeBlock.answer: Chrome may warn the file 'is not commonly downloaded'. Click 'Keep' in the download bar at the bottom.

windowsSmartScreen.question: Windows says 'Windows protected your PC'?
windowsSmartScreen.answer: This is Windows SmartScreen's default prompt for new apps. Click 'More info', then 'Run anyway'. Overleap is EV code-signed and this prompt won't appear after installation.

macosGatekeeper.question: macOS says 'cannot verify the developer'?
macosGatekeeper.answer: After first install, go to System Settings then Privacy and Security, find the Overleap prompt at the bottom, and click 'Open Anyway'. Subsequent launches won't show this. For VPN mode, you'll be guided to allow the system extension.

androidInstallBlock.question: Android phone blocks APK installation?
androidInstallBlock.answer: Some Android phone brands (Huawei, Xiaomi, OPPO, vivo, etc.) run cloud-based security checks during APK installation that may block non-store apps. Solution: after downloading the APK, turn on Airplane Mode before installing. Install offline, then turn off Airplane Mode after completion.

androidUsbInstall.question: Android install blocked and need help?
androidUsbInstall.answer: Open the Overleap desktop app, go to Account → Other Device Install → Android Phone Install, connect your phone via USB cable, and follow the wizard to install automatically.

security.question: Is Overleap safe?
security.answer: Overleap is Apple notarized and Windows EV code-signed. All installers are distributed via HTTPS with verifiable SHA-256 checksums. The k2 protocol core is under an independent security audit programme.

install.routerSubtitle: Install Overleap on OpenWrt, x86 soft router, or embedded Linux — every device on your LAN goes through the tunnel automatically.
install.routerCliHint: Paste this into your router SSH session. The installer detects your architecture and registers a startup service.

openwrt.title: OpenWrt routers
openwrt.body: Registers as a procd service with optional LuCI integration. Supports ARMv7, arm64, x86_64.

softrouter.title: Debian / Ubuntu soft router
softrouter.body: Registers as a systemd unit. Works on N100 mini-PCs, iStoreOS, ImmortalWrt derivatives.

sbc.title: Generic Linux SBC
sbc.body: Raspberry Pi, Orange Pi, N1 boxes — installer auto-detects the distribution and wires up the daemon.

routerNextSteps.title: After install
routerNextSteps.step1: Open http://<router-ip>:1779 in your browser to reach the gateway dashboard.
routerNextSteps.step2: Paste your subscription URL (k2subs://) or a k2v5:// node URL.
routerNextSteps.step3: Click connect — every device on the LAN flows through k2.
